spec_match.rationale: Uses `::column` on a real multi-column container (`columns: 3`), toggles only post-layout properties (background, column-rule, scroll-snap-align), feature-detects via `CSS.supports('selector(::column)')`.

interactive.rationale: Three independent toggle buttons drive live `::column` rule injection plus a synthesized CSS readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises toggling background tint, scroll-snap target, and column-rule with live CSS — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't demonstrate the post-layout-only constraint (setting `width` or `margin` on `::column` is a spec no-op).

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5192332683771904 and CSS Overflow Level 5 spec.

design_system.rationale: Page styles use variables, but synthesized CSS-out string embeds raw `#0022ff`, `#fff`, `#f4f3ee` literals.

openQuestions.title: Sort out button label / state mapping
openQuestions.detail: Button id `t-rule` is labelled `background: tint` but maps to state `tinted`; `t-divider` maps to `divider`. Confusing.

openQuestions.title: Add a no-op property demo
openQuestions.detail: Toggle that sets `width: 100px` on `::column` and shows layout is unchanged — teaches the most important constraint.

openQuestions.title: Use design-system variables in the CSS readout
openQuestions.detail: Generated CSS contains `#0022ff`, `#fff`, `#f4f3ee`. Replace with var(--accent-blue), var(--bg-paper), var(--bg-stone).

openQuestions.title: Show horizontal-scroll carousel use case
openQuestions.detail: Carousel use case is horizontal scroll with column fragments. This demo uses vertical snap. Add horizontal variant — that's the spec's emphasis.

summary: Solid baseline demo. Biggest miss: not teaching the post-layout-properties-only constraint.
